bugs.error_message: AGENTS.md violation: inline type import `typeof import('node:fs/promises').readdir` used in test mock cast
bugs.root_cause: Inline dynamic imports used in type positions are explicitly forbidden by AGENTS.md. The mock was cast using `as unknown as typeof import('node:fs/promises').readdir` instead of a named type import.
bugs.fix: Added `import type { readdir as ReaddirFn } from 'node:fs/promises'` at top of test file; replaced all inline type casts with `as unknown as typeof ReaddirFn`.

bugs.error_message: scanReposInRoot options used `typeof readdir` (broad overloaded type) requiring forced casts in tests
bugs.root_cause: node:fs/promises `readdir` has many overloads making the type impractical to mock without ugly casts. The options interface used the full overloaded type.
bugs.fix: Introduced purpose-built `ReaddirWithFileTypes` type alias and `ScanReposOptions` interface. The real `readdir` is wrapped in a small lambda that narrows the return type. Tests now use `as unknown as typeof ReaddirFn` (named import) instead of `as unknown as typeof import(...).readdir`.

bugs.error_message: createSubtaskWorktree silently swallowed symlink errors along with missing .env — incorrect error handling
bugs.root_cause: Single try/catch around both `access()` and `symlink()` swallowed symlink failures, leaving the worktree in an inconsistent state without any signal to the caller.
bugs.fix: Split into two blocks: access() failure sets envExists=false (silent skip); symlink() runs outside the catch and propagates errors to the caller.

bugs.error_message: Cannot find module '../../../src/jira/jira-board-state.js' (TS diagnostic in jira-board-state.test.ts line 43)
bugs.root_cause: Import used .js extension which is wrong for this project's moduleResolution=Bundler tsconfig. Bundler resolution does not allow .js suffix on TypeScript source imports.

bugs.error_message: 'beforeEach' is declared but its value is never read (jira-api.test.ts line 1)
bugs.root_cause: beforeEach was imported from vitest but not used in any test suite in jira-api.test.ts.
bugs.fix: Removed beforeEach from the vitest import statement.

bugs.error_message: 'await' has no effect on the type of this expression (runtime-server.ts line 249)
bugs.root_cause: trpcHttpHandler is http.RequestListener (returns void), not a Promise. createHTTPHandler from @trpc/server/adapters/standalone returns a synchronous request listener.
bugs.fix: Removed unnecessary await before trpcHttpHandler(req, res) call.

bugs.error_message: fetchIssue unsafe cast: `raw as { key?: ... }` does not guard against non-object values
bugs.root_cause: callJiraMcp returns `unknown`; direct `as` cast without runtime type check is unsafe when raw is null, string, or number.
bugs.fix: Added runtime guard: `raw !== null && typeof raw === 'object' ? (raw as ...) : {}`

bugs.error_message: getJiraProjectKey in CreateJiraApiDependencies was unused — never called inside createJiraApi
bugs.root_cause: Dependency was added to the interface but never consumed; test mock also referenced it.
bugs.fix: Removed getJiraProjectKey from CreateJiraApiDependencies interface, from createJiraApi call in runtime-server.ts, and from jira-api.test.ts mock.

bugs.error_message: startSubtaskSession called loadJiraSubtasks twice — once at start and again after startTaskSession
bugs.root_cause: Second call was a defensive re-fetch before writing status, but unnecessary because lockedFileSystem.writeJsonFileAtomic handles atomic writes; the first call's result is still valid.
bugs.fix: Removed second loadJiraSubtasks call; reuse first call's `subtasks` map, mutate subtasks[subtaskId] directly, then write atomically.

bugs.fix: Rewrote 16→3 lines (12 removed) | Also: handleShowStartupOnboardingDialog,; onOpenStartupOnboardingDialog: handleOpenStartupOn | Also: <StartupOnboardingDialog; open={isStartupOnboardingDialogOpen}

bugs.error_message: Import To-Do button does nothing, no backend log
bugs.root_cause: claude -p --output-format json wraps text in { type, result: "string" }. importFromJira only checked Array.isArray(raw.result) — always false when result is a string. issues stayed [] and 0 imports returned silently.
bugs.fix: Added branch: when raw.result is a string, JSON.parse it and use if it's an array.

bugs.error_message: claude -p exited with code 1: Warning: no stdin data received in 3s, proceeding without it.
bugs.root_cause: execFile('claude') inherits parent's stdin (a TTY/pipe). Claude CLI waits for stdin input, times out after 3s, exits code 1.
bugs.fix: Switched from execFile+promisify to spawn with stdio: ['ignore','pipe','pipe'] — stdin ignored, no TTY wait.

bugs.root_cause: Used "??" instead of "==="

bugs.error_message: claude -p exited with code 1: (empty stderr) — Import To-Do fails
bugs.root_cause: --max-turns 1 is too low: claude uses turn 1 to call the Atlassian MCP tool, needs turn 2 to process the result and return JSON. Hits max turns, exits code 1 with subtype error_max_turns, empty stderr.
bugs.fix: Changed --max-turns 1 to --max-turns 5. Also improved error handling: parse JSON even on non-zero exit to extract is_error/errors for clearer messages.

bugs.root_cause: Had `assignee = currentUser() AND status = "

bugs.error_message: importFromJira returns 0 cards — claude wraps result in markdown fences, JSON.parse fails
bugs.root_cause:
claude -p returns result field as ```json
[...]
``` despite prompt saying no other text. JSON.parse fails on backtick fences → issues = [].
bugs.fix: Strip markdown code fences from rawObj.result before JSON.parse. Also improved prompt: specify searchJiraIssuesUsingJql tool name, explicitly say no markdown/backticks.

bugs.error_message: importFromJira returns 0 cards — JQL status = "To Do" too narrow
bugs.root_cause: JQL used status = "To Do" which requires exact Jira status name match. Users may have status named Backlog, Open, etc. in the To Do category.
bugs.fix: Changed to statusCategory = "To Do" which matches all statuses in the To Do category regardless of name.

bugs.error_message: claude -p failed: Reached maximum number of turns (5)
bugs.root_cause: Subprocess spawned from project cwd inherited CLAUDE.md (OpenWolf), AGENTS.md, project .claude/settings.json hooks (SessionStart/PreToolUse), and user-level superpowers SessionStart injection. These consumed 3-5 turns before the agent could call a single MCP tool. With --max-turns 5 there was no room left for the final JSON response.
bugs.fix: Spawn with cwd=os.tmpdir() (no project CLAUDE.md/hooks load), add --disable-slash-commands (kills skill invocations), add --allowedTools restricted to 5 Atlassian MCP tools, add --append-system-prompt to anchor agent behavior, bump --max-turns 5→10. Also log session_id+num_turns in error message for forensic debugging.

bugs.error_message: POL-4504 not fetched by importFromJira despite being assigned to currentUser()
bugs.root_cause: JIRA_STATUS_MAP only had 'todo' (no hyphen) but Jira returns status name 'To-Do' which lowercases to 'to-do'. Also missing 'in progress' (space) — Jira default is 'In Progress' not 'In-Progress'. Issues with unmapped status are silently skipped (new) or removed (existing).
bugs.fix: Added 'to do', 'to-do', 'in progress', and 'done' to JIRA_STATUS_MAP to cover Jira's real default status names.
